message:
test(glyph_atlas): add 34 edge-case tests for glyph atlas cache (bd-1ablp)

Cover error paths (InvalidRaster, GlyphTooLarge, AtlasFull), Display/Error
impls, zero-budget cache, single-pixel rasters, type defaults, accessor
coverage, placement field checks, sequential evictions, objective formula
edge cases, and GlyphKey construction. 11 → 45 total tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -148,6 +148,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-19x","title":"Create performance benchmark suite","description":"TESTING - PERFORMANCE BENCHMARKS

## Background
FrankenTUI has strict performance budgets from the plan. Benchmarks must verify these budgets are met and prevent regressions.

## Performance Budgets (from plan)

### Core Operations
| Operation | Target | Panic Threshold |
|-----------|--------|-----------------|
| Cell comparison | < 1ns | < 5ns |
| Cell bits_eq SIMD | < 0.5ns | < 2ns |
| Row comparison (80 cols) | < 100ns | < 500ns |
| Buffer diff (80×24) | < 10µs | < 50µs |

### Present Operations
| Scenario | p50 Target | p99 Target |
|----------|------------|------------|
| 80×24, 5% changed | < 1.0ms | < 3.0ms |
| 80×24, 50% changed | < 2.0ms | < 5.0ms |
| 200×50, 5% changed | < 2.0ms | < 5.0ms |
| 200×50, full redraw | < 10ms | < 25ms |

### Layout Operations
| Operation | Target | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| Flex solve (10 items) | < 50µs | |
| Flex solve (50 items) | < 200µs | |
| Grid solve (10×10) | < 500µs | |

### Widget Rendering
| Widget | Target (per instance) |
|--------|----------------------|
| Block | < 50µs |
| Paragraph (100 chars) | < 100µs |
| Table (100 rows visible) | < 500µs |

## Technical Requirements

### Benchmark Infrastructure
- Use criterion crate
- benches/ directory structure
- Statistical analysis with confidence intervals
- Comparison against baseline

### Benchmark Organization
benches/
├── cell_bench.rs      # Cell operations
├── buffer_bench.rs    # Buffer operations  
├── diff_bench.rs      # Diff algorithms
├── present_bench.rs   # Presenter operations
├── layout_bench.rs    # Layout solving
├── widget_bench.rs    # Widget rendering
└── e2e_bench.rs       # Full pipeline

### Each Benchmark Must
1. Warm up properly
2. Use black_box to prevent optimization
3. Test multiple input sizes
4. Report memory allocation counts
5. Compare against budget threshold

### Benchmark Example
use criterion::{black_box, criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion};

fn cell_comparison(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let cell1 = Cell::default();
    let cell2 = Cell::char('a');
    
    c.bench_function("cell_bits_eq_same", |b| {
        b.iter(|| black_box(&cell1).bits_eq(black_box(&cell1)))
    });
    
    c.bench_function("cell_bits_eq_diff", |b| {
        b.iter(|| black_box(&cell1).bits_eq(black_box(&cell2)))
    });
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cell comparison benchmarks
- [ ] Buffer operation benchmarks
- [ ] Diff algorithm benchmarks
- [ ] Present pipeline benchmarks
- [ ] Layout solver benchmarks
- [ ] Widget render benchmarks
- [ ] Budget verification in CI
- [ ] Baseline comparison reports
- [ ] Memory allocation tracking

 {"id":"bd-1abx","title":"Update widget tests to work with Frame-based API","description":"## Scope

Update all tests in ftui-widgets to work with the new Frame-based Widget API.

## Test Changes Required

### Creating test Frames

Tests that create a Buffer directly need to create a Frame instead:

```rust
// BEFORE:
let mut buf = Buffer::new(80, 24);
widget.render(area, &mut buf);

// AFTER:
let mut pool = GraphemePool::new();
let mut frame = Frame::new(80, 24, &mut pool);
widget.render(area, &mut frame);
// Access buffer for assertions:
assert_eq!(frame.buffer.get(0, 0).symbol, "X");
```

### Test helper pattern

Consider adding a test helper:

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
fn test_frame(width: u16, height: u16) -> (Frame<'static>, GraphemePool) {
    // Use leaked pool for static lifetime in tests
    let pool = Box::leak(Box::new(GraphemePool::new()));
    let frame = Frame::new(width, height, pool);
    (frame, pool)
}
```

Or use a simpler pattern with local pool:

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
macro_rules! with_frame {
    ($width:expr, $height:expr, |$frame:ident| $body:block) => {{
        let mut pool = GraphemePool::new();
        let mut $frame = Frame::new($width, $height, &mut pool);
        $body
    }};
}
```

## Files to Update

All test modules in:
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/*.rs (inline tests)
- crates/ftui-widgets/tests/*.rs (integration tests, if any)

## Important

- Don't change test LOGIC, only test SETUP
- Tests should still verify the same behaviors
- Buffer content assertions now use `frame.buffer`

## Verification

- `cargo test -p ftui-widgets` must pass
- All existing test coverage maintained","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:23:20.592207371Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:29:50.735648159Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:29:50.735627189Z","close_reason":"All widget tests updated to use Frame-based API. Tests create GraphemePool, create Frame from pool, call widget.render(area, &mut frame), and assert on frame.buffer. All 275 ftui-widgets tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1abx","depends_on_id":"bd-10y0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1abx","depends_on_id":"bd-1rqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1abx","depends_on_id":"bd-2ua8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1abx","depends_on_id":"bd-f38d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1abx","depends_on_id":"bd-z8q6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ai1","title":"SDK modularization strategy (embedded vs WASM/FFI)","description":"Design a crate split strategy for future embedded/FFI targets to avoid C‑FFI bridging across languages.

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: SDK modularization strategy (embedded vs WASM/FFI)
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:15:19.856645839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:20:26.821068754Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:20:26.821045991Z","close_reason":"Completed strategy docs (embedded-core, FFI layout, placeholder policy, test strategy, ADR-007)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","ffi","sdk"],"comments":[{"id":298,"issue_id":"bd-1ai1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background
- Feedback suggests a clean separation between an “embedded core” crate that abstracts IO and host‑specific FFI crates (wasm, C, Zig, JVM, etc.).
- Goal is to prevent fragile “C‑FFI as universal bridge” architectures.

Scope
- This is a future‑facing design track; no code changes until the architecture is clear.

Deliverables
- A crate naming and dependency plan that makes each host binding a first‑class target.
- Guidance on how to keep the embedded core pure and host‑agnostic.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:15:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ai1.1","title":"Define embedded core boundaries","description":"Identify which functionality must be host‑agnostic and IO‑abstracted for an embedded core crate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Define embedded core boundaries
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:15:30.494054050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:27:15.724013995Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T06:27:15.723989188Z","close_reason":"Defined embedded core boundaries in docs/spec/embedded-core.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1ai1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":299,"issue_id":"bd-1ai1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Boundary Criteria
- IO should be abstracted behind traits (stdin/stdout, timing, filesystem).
- Core logic should have no direct terminal or platform bindings.
- Decide whether layout/render belongs in embedded core or remains host‑specific.

Deliverable
- A list of modules/types proposed for embedded core vs host adapters.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.6.5","title":"Fix ws_client hash_key/geometry coherence for structured frame overrides","description":"Goal: ensure tests/e2e/lib/ws_client.py emits coherent frame records when structured WS envelopes override mode/cols/rows but omit hash_key. Currently hash_key can remain stale if cols/rows are overridden after key computation. Implement deterministic key derivation from effective mode+geometry unless explicit hash_key is provided, and add focused self-tests for these cases. Validation: python3 tests/e2e/lib/ws_client.py --self-test; python3 -m py_compile tests/e2e/lib/ws_client.py; cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggySparrow","created_at":"2026-02-09T09:52:21.895335812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T09:54:18.213361542Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T09:54:18.213341525Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T09:52:21.895335812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Change SessionRecorder jsonl open mode to truncate/write and add a self-test proving stale preexisting content is removed and only current-run events remain. Validation: python3 tests/e2e/lib/ws_client.py --self-test; python3 -m py_compile tests/e2e/lib/ws_client.py; cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggySparrow","created_at":"2026-02-09T09:55:53.638307129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T09:57:16.016251213Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T09:57:16.016231656Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T09:55:53.638307129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.7","title":"Determinism: record/replay web sessions (input + resize + ticks)","description":"Goal
- Ensure we can deterministically reproduce bugs and perf issues in the browser.

Requirements
- Record: input events, resize events, tick deltas, and any nondeterminism sources.
- Replay: drive ftui-web runner to exact same rendered output checksums.

Acceptance Criteria
- A recorded session replays to identical frame checksums on same build.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.8","title":"Test: wasm-bindgen-test coverage for ftui-web runner","description":"Goal
- Add WASM-side tests for core event and rendering flows.

Acceptance Criteria
- Headless tests validate update/view invariants.
- A small set of end-to-end render checksums run under wasm test runner.
